breaking_news.title: Apple issues urgent lock screen warnings for active iOS exploits
breaking_news.summary:
Apple has begun issuing urgent lock screen warnings to users of outdated iPhones and iPads regarding active web-based exploits. The notifications appear as critical software alerts from the Settings app, urging immediate updates to protect against ongoing attacks targeting older iOS versions.

This move highlights a significant escalation in active exploitation campaigns targeting mobile devices beyond the officially supported software lifecycle. Users running older versions like iOS 17.0 are now at direct risk, necessitating immediate patching to prevent potential device compromise.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters claims 350GB data breach at European Commission
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack on its cloud infrastructure hosting Europa.eu websites, resulting in the theft of over 350GB of data. The threat actor group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility, alleging access to mail servers, databases, and confidential internal communications.

This breach impacts the EU's executive body and potentially affects Union entities notified of the incident, raising concerns about data sovereignty and cloud security. Although the Commission contained the incident quickly, the volume of stolen data suggests significant exposure of sensitive government information.

breaking_news.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI Director's personal email
breaking_news.summary:
Iran-linked hackers from the Handala Hack Team successfully breached the personal Gmail account of FBI Director Kash Patel, leaking photos and documents. The group claimed the breach on their website, stating that the FBI's security was inadequate while the FBI confirmed the incident involved historical personal data.

This high-profile attack targets a prominent U.S. law enforcement figure, signaling a shift towards targeting individual leaders rather than just organizational networks. While no classified government information was exposed, the incident underscores the vulnerability of personal accounts belonging to high-value targets.

breaking_news.title: TA446 deploys DarkSword iOS exploit kit in spear-phishing campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Russian state-sponsored threat group TA446 is deploying the DarkSword iOS exploit kit in targeted spear-phishing campaigns against iOS devices. Proofpoint disclosed that the actors are leveraging recently disclosed vulnerabilities to compromise iPhones through malicious email attachments.

This activity represents a significant threat to mobile security, as the exploit kit is designed to bypass standard iOS protections and gain persistent access. Organizations and individuals using iOS devices must remain vigilant against phishing attempts, as the campaign targets specific high-value users.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP compromises Telnyx PyPI package with hidden malware
breaking_news.summary:
The TeamPCP threat actor compromised the Telnyx package on the Python Package Index, uploading malicious versions that deliver credential-stealing malware. The attack hid the malicious payload within a WAV audio file, bypassing standard detection mechanisms for Python packages.

This supply chain attack threatens developers relying on the Telnyx library, potentially exposing sensitive credentials and API keys across multiple environments. The use of obfuscation techniques like WAV files highlights the evolving sophistication of supply chain compromises targeting developer tooling.

breaking_news.title: CISA adds F5 BIG-IP AMP RCE flaw to KEV catalog
breaking_news.summary:
CISA added a critical flaw in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, CVE-2025-53521, allows remote code execution when specific malicious traffic triggers an access policy on a virtual server.

This reclassification from a Denial-of-Service issue to a critical RCE flaw significantly increases the risk for organizations using F5 infrastructure. Immediate patching is required to prevent attackers from gaining full control over critical network security appliances.

breaking_news.title: Langflow RCE vulnerability actively exploited within hours
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers have actively exploited a critical Remote Code Execution vulnerability in the Langflow AI framework within hours of its disclosure. CISA added CVE-2026-33017 to its KEV catalog after observing attackers weaponizing the flaw to execute arbitrary code without authentication.

This rapid exploitation highlights the vulnerability of AI development tools, where unauthenticated endpoints can lead to full system compromise. Organizations using Langflow for AI workflows must urgently update their instances to prevent unauthorized access and data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: Red Menshen APT deploys stealthy implants in telecom networks
breaking_news.summary:
China-linked APT group Red Menshen has deployed stealthy BPFDoor implants within telecom networks to conduct long-term espionage against government targets. Rapid7 Labs uncovered the campaign, which infiltrates critical infrastructure to monitor communications and maintain hidden access.

Compromising telecom networks threatens entire populations by exposing call records and sensitive communications across multiple countries. The use of kernel-level implants makes detection extremely difficult, posing a severe risk to national security and critical infrastructure integrity.

breaking_news.title: CISA warns of critical PTC Windchill flaw with no patch
breaking_news.summary:
CISA and BSI issued warnings about a critical vulnerability in PTC Windchill and FlexPLM software with a CVSS score of 10.0. The flaw allows remote code execution through deserialization of untrusted data, prompting German police to physically warn organizations of imminent exploitation.

With no patch currently available, organizations managing product lifecycle data face significant risk of system compromise and data theft. The severity of the flaw necessitates immediate implementation of mitigations to prevent attackers from executing arbitrary code on vulnerable servers.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS exploit kit linked to Operation Triangulation
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers identified the Coruna iOS exploit kit as an evolution of the framework used in the 2023 Operation Triangulation espionage campaign. The kit targets iPhones running iOS versions 13.0 through 17.2.1 using updated kernel exploits previously seen in state-sponsored attacks.

This development suggests a resurgence of sophisticated mobile espionage tools, potentially linked to the same actors behind previous high-profile iOS compromises. Users on affected iOS versions are at risk of zero-click or targeted attacks that bypass standard security controls.
